title: Greenhouse Screening to Combat Downy Mildew in Pearl Millet. An ICRISAT Success Story
abstract:
Downy mildew, caused by the fungus
Sclerospora graminicola, is the most devastating
disease of pearl millet in Asia and Africa. The
disease is more severe in single-cross hybrids
than in open-pollinated varieties.
funders: United States Agency for International Development
